title: Bath buns
subtitle: Enriched sweet buns with mixed peel, caraway, and a sugar crust
excerpt: Rich, pillowy buns from Bath, made with an enriched dough packed with mixed peel and caraway seeds, glazed with egg, and topped with crushed sugar. The caraway seeds and peel are the defining flavours. Makes twelve.
sourceNotes: Bath buns are a British enriched bun associated with the city of Bath, documented from the early eighteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: Bath buns are recorded as a rich yeast bun made with eggs, butter, sugar, caraway seeds, and candied peel, baked until pale gold and finished with a sugar wash. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: an enriched bun made with a light yeast dough, eggs, and candied peel is documented as a Bath speciality, distinguishing them from the plainer London bun.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze cooled buns in a bag for up to 1 month. Defrost at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best on the day they are baked. Warm briefly in a low oven if eating the next day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the buns should be pale gold with a thin caramelised crust from the sugar; 18–22 minutes

content.text: The dough is enriched enough to be slightly sticky; resist flouring the work surface heavily during shaping or the crumb will tighten. The caraway seeds and mixed peel are the flavour signature of a Bath bun; neither can be reduced without changing the character of the bake. The crushed sugar on top gives a faint crunch on the crust and a pleasant sweetness without being iced.

Combine the {{strong-bread-flour}}, {{yeast-fast-action}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} in a large bowl. Add the warm {{whole-milk}} and {{eggs}} and mix to a shaggy dough. Turn out and knead for 5 minutes until smooth. Add the {{unsalted-butter}} a little at a time, kneading after each addition. Continue for 3 to 4 minutes until the butter is fully incorporated. Shape into a ball, place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and leave for 60 minutes until doubled.

Turn the dough out and flatten lightly. Scatter the {{mixed-peel}} and {{caraway-seeds}} over the surface. Fold and knead briefly to distribute evenly. Divide into 12 pieces of about 80 g each. Roll each piece into a ball and place on a lined baking tray, spaced well apart. Cover loosely and prove for 45 minutes until noticeably puffed. Preheat the oven to 190°C fan.
Brush the proved buns gently with the beaten {{eggs}} glaze. Scatter the {{demerara-sugar}} over the tops. Bake for 18 to 22 minutes until pale gold. Transfer to a rack and eat warm.
